subtopics: Complete the square for a quadratic polynomial ax^2 + bx + c and use completed square form
Find the discriminant of a quadratic polynomial and use it to determine the number of real roots
Solve quadratic equations by factorisation, completing the square and using the quadratic formula
Solve quadratic inequalities in one unknown
Solve by substitution a pair of simultaneous equations where one is linear and one is quadratic
Recognise and solve equations in x which are quadratic in some function of x
topics.description: Candidates should be able to carry out completing the square, use the discriminant, solve quadratic equations and inequalities, solve simultaneous equations (one linear, one quadratic), and solve equations that are quadratic in some function of x.

subtopics: Understand the terms function, domain, range, one-one function, inverse function and composition of functions
Identify the range of a given function in simple cases
Find the composition of two given functions including the condition for a composite function gf to be formed
Determine whether or not a given function is one-one
Find the inverse of a one-one function in simple cases
Illustrate in graphical terms the relation between a one-one function and its inverse including the mirror line y = x
Understand and use transformations of the graph y = f(x) given by y = f(x) + a, y = f(x + a), y = af(x), y = f(ax) and simple combinations
topics.description: Candidates should be able to understand function terminology, identify ranges, find compositions and inverses, sketch inverse function graphs with reflection in y = x, and apply transformations including translations, reflections and stretches.

subtopics: Find the equation of a straight line given sufficient information (two points or one point and the gradient)
Interpret and use the forms y = mx + c, y - y1 = m(x - x1), ax + by + c = 0 in solving problems
Calculate distances, gradients, midpoints, points of intersection and use the relationship between gradients of parallel and perpendicular lines
Understand that the equation (x - a)^2 + (y - b)^2 = r^2 represents a circle with centre (a, b) and radius r including the expanded form
Use algebraic methods to solve problems involving lines and circles including elementary geometrical properties of circles
Understand the relationship between a graph and its associated algebraic equation and use the relationship between points of intersection and solutions of equations
topics.description: Candidates should be able to work with straight lines in various forms, calculate distances, gradients and midpoints, work with circle equations in standard and expanded form, solve line-circle problems using geometrical properties, and relate graph intersections to equation solutions.

subtopics: Understand the definition of a radian and use the relationship between radians and degrees
Use the formulae s = r*theta and A = (1/2)*r^2*theta for arc length and sector area of a circle
Solve problems concerning arc length and sector area including calculation of lengths and angles in triangles and areas of triangles
topics.description: Candidates should be able to understand radians and their relationship to degrees, and use the arc length and sector area formulae to solve problems involving circles and triangles.

subtopics: Sketch and use graphs of sine, cosine and tangent functions for angles of any size in degrees or radians
Use the exact values of sine, cosine and tangent of 30 degrees, 45 degrees, 60 degrees and related angles
Use the notations sin^-1(x), cos^-1(x), tan^-1(x) to denote principal values of inverse trigonometric relations
Use the identities tan(theta) = sin(theta)/cos(theta) and sin^2(theta) + cos^2(theta) = 1 in proving identities, simplifying expressions and solving equations
Find all solutions of simple trigonometrical equations lying in a specified interval
topics.description: Candidates should be able to sketch trig graphs, use exact values for standard angles, work with inverse trig notation, apply fundamental trig identities, and solve simple trigonometrical equations within a given interval.

subtopics: Use the expansion of (a + b)^n where n is a positive integer including the notations nCr and n factorial
Recognise arithmetic and geometric progressions
Use the formulae for the nth term and for the sum of the first n terms to solve problems involving arithmetic or geometric progressions
Use the condition for the convergence of a geometric progression
Use the formula for the sum to infinity of a convergent geometric progression
topics.description: Candidates should be able to use the binomial expansion for positive integer powers, recognise and work with arithmetic and geometric progressions including nth term and sum formulae, and find sums to infinity for convergent geometric series.

subtopics: Understand the gradient of a curve at a point as the limit of the gradients of a sequence of chords using the notations f'(x), f''(x), dy/dx and d^2y/dx^2
Use the derivative of x^n for any rational n together with constant multiples, sums and differences of functions
Differentiate composite functions using the chain rule
Apply differentiation to gradients, tangents and normals
Apply differentiation to increasing and decreasing functions and rates of change including connected rates of change
Locate stationary points and determine their nature using the second derivative
Use information about stationary points in sketching graphs
topics.description: Candidates should be able to understand differentiation as a limit, differentiate powers, sums, differences and composite functions using the chain rule, apply differentiation to gradients, tangents, normals, rates of change, and use stationary points to determine curve shape.

subtopics: Understand integration as the reverse process of differentiation
Integrate (ax + b)^n for any rational n except -1 together with constant multiples, sums and differences
Solve problems involving the evaluation of a constant of integration
Evaluate definite integrals including simple cases of improper integrals
Use definite integration to find the area of a region bounded by a curve and lines parallel to the axes, or between a curve and a line or between two curves
Use definite integration to find a volume of revolution about one of the axes
topics.description: Candidates should be able to integrate as the reverse of differentiation, evaluate definite and indefinite integrals including finding constants of integration, and use definite integration to calculate areas between curves and lines and volumes of revolution.
